elements.sent-2411-001: The Fermi surface topology of a triple non-hermitian (NH) Weyl semimetal (WSM) driven by bi-circularly polarized light is presented in this study.
elements.sent-2411-002: A NH WSM in particular has remarkable outlines.
elements.sent-2411-003: Bi-circular light, however, modifies the symmetry features of non-hermitian triple Weyl and causes an unusual new kind of band swapping.
elements.sent-2411-004: We observe swapping between the imaginary bands (with or without exceptional degenaracies), which causes unique Fermi surfaces in the form of double rings and knots.
elements.sent-2411-005: This is something never discussed before phase transition between Weyl and knotted phases.
elements.sent-2411-006: We also discuss the corresponding changes in the Berry curvature as well.
elements.sent-2411-007: The search for new and distinct topological phases has long been at the forefront of research.
elements.sent-2411-008: Examples include the topological insulator phase (TI) \cite{Hasan, Kane , nodal line semimetals, Dirac and Weyl-semimetal phases (WSM) \cite{Vafek, Burkov, Armitage , and so on.}}
elements.sent-2411-009: The TIs are the first instance of the materials displaying unique edge states \cite{Kane , even though the majority of the sample is insulating.}
elements.sent-2411-010: The other semimetallic phases mentioned here provide a three-dimensional example \cite{Burkov, Armitage .}
elements.sent-2411-011: Different properties of the 3D WSMs have been remarkably studied in the literature \cite{Vafek, Burkov, Armitage .}
elements.sent-2411-012: The Weyl point, also known as the source or sink of the Berry curvature, is where the two bands in WSM intersect.
elements.sent-2411-013: In this instance, the dispersion is linear, with a topological charge of ±1.
elements.sent-2411-014: Conversely, higher topological charge WSMs (i.e., $\pm 2$ or $\pm 3$) are found in nature.
elements.sent-2411-015: These are the multi-WSM (double or triple WSM) \cite{Gupta, MW2, MW1, TW, Fang .}
elements.sent-2411-016: The rotational symmetry of the point group $C_{n}$ is preserved in multi-weyl having a nonlinear dispersion \cite{Our 2023, Our,Huang .}
elements.sent-2411-017: WSMs with a $\pm 1$ charge are extensively studied, but multi-WSMs have recently gained interest.
elements.sent-2411-018: The scope to find new topological phases in this unique class of materials is thus still active.
elements.sent-2411-019: Its study must illustrate the various characteristics of hermitian and non-hermitian multi-WSMs, further driven by time-periodic fields.
elements.sent-2411-020: The light-induced topological phases greatly aid the comprehension of topological phase transitions \cite{f1,f2,f3,f4,f5,f6,f7,f8,f9,f10,f11,f12,f13 .}
elements.sent-2411-021: The Floquet mechanism is observed in topological materials when time-periodic fields are present.
elements.sent-2411-022: Previous research addresses light-induced topological phases when both linear and circular polarisations are present \cite{Our 2023, Our .}
elements.sent-2411-023: Remarkably, the frequency and amplitude of the light serve as the tuning parameters for the various phases.
elements.sent-2411-024: Furthermore, the distinct Fermi surface topology—which we refer to as Lifshitz transitions—is another feature of the light-induced topological phases \cite{f13a, f13b, f13c, f13d, f13e .}
elements.sent-2411-025: Though Floquet topological systems with circular or linear polarizations are widely addressed in the literature, bi-circular polarization-induced Floquet dynamics have just been covered in \cite{bicircular, graphene .}
elements.sent-2411-026: Furthermore, this unique polarization selectively breaks spatial symmetry as inversion.

elements.sent-2411-100: In this paper, we have analysed a dissipated (gain/loss) triple WSM in the presence of a bi-circularly polarized light.
elements.sent-2411-101: The magic of this particular polarization is that it provides better tunability by providing three tuning parameter angles.
elements.sent-2411-102: This kind of light provides spatial symmetry breaking in the crystal and is thus responsible for unique topological phases.
elements.sent-2411-103: The system we choose is the triple WSM, which has a $C_{6}$ rotational symmetry having a non-linear dispersion.
elements.sent-2411-104: However, once the bi-circular light is switched on, the light breaks spatial inversion and in a combined effect of non-hermiticity and the light, we eventually land up in a double nodal ring and knotted semimetals.

elements.sent-2411-110: The Fermi surface topology of a triple non-hermitian (NH) Weyl-semimetal (WSM) driven by bi-circularly (BCL) polarized light is presented in this study.
elements.sent-2411-111: BCL light, however, modifies the symmetry features of NH triple Weyl and causes an unusual new kind of band swapping.
elements.sent-2411-112: We observe swapping between the imaginary bands (with or without exceptional degenaracies), which causes unique Fermi surfaces in the form of double rings and knots .
elements.sent-2411-113: Examples include the topological insulator (TI) \cite{Hasan, Kane , nodal line semimetals, Dirac and Weyl-semimetal phases (WSM) \cite{Vafek, Burkov, Armitage , and so on.}}
elements.sent-2411-114: The Weyl point, the source or sink of the Berry curvature, is the place where the two bands in WSM intersect.
elements.sent-2411-115: In this instance, the dispersion is linear, with a topological charge of $\pm 1$.
elements.sent-2411-116: WSMs with a $\pm 1$ charge are extensively studied\cite{Zhong,Armitage,NN,Scanlon , but multi-WSMs have recently gained interest.}
elements.sent-2411-117: Its study must illustrate the various characteristics of hermitian and NH multi-WSMs, further driven by time-periodic fields.

elements.sent-2411-131: The present paper considers a BCL-polarised light-induced NH triple WSM, and the energy dispersion shows a unique Fermi surface topology.
elements.sent-2411-132: Fig. 1 shows a glimpse of the discussed Fermi surfaces (double nodal ring (Fig. 1(a)) and self-linked (Fig. 1(b))) in the paper.
elements.sent-2411-133: The novelty of our work lies in the fact that the topology of a NH triple WSM is tuned by the application of BCL light.
elements.sent-2411-134: The topological nature of Weyl systems in the presence of non-hermiticity has been discussed previously in the literature \cite{Our 2023, Our .}
elements.sent-2411-135: However, the topological phases we find in this work are hard to achieve with periodic circularly/linearly polarized light, even in the presence of non-hermiticity (this fact is discussed in appendix B for single WSM in presence of BCL and a triple WSM with a circular polarized light).
elements.sent-2411-136: The tuning of various topological NH phases with the different angles of the BCL light is something new and stands out from the line of work in NH topology.

elements.sent-2411-143: As the next step, we would like to investigate the role of BCL polarized light on the triple WSM.
elements.sent-2411-144: BCL appears due to the superposition of two circularly polarized lights having two opposite chiralities and different frequencies of an integer multiple of $\eta$ and maintaining a phase difference $\alpha.$ The polarization field induced by a BCL light is \cite{bicircular \begin{equation}A(t)=A_0\sqrt{2}\,Re\left[e^{-i(\eta\Omega t-\alpha)}\epsilon_{R}+e^{-i\omega t}\epsilon_{L}\right]\,, \label{2}\end{equation} where $A_0$ is the amplitude and $\bm{\epsilon _{L/R}$ are left (L) and right (R) circularly polarized (CL) basis vectors.}}

elements.sent-2411-210: In this paper, we have analysed a NH triple WSM in the presence of a BCL polarized light.
elements.sent-2411-211: This kind of polarization offers better tunability and provides modification in spatial inversion and rotational symmetry of the crystal.
elements.sent-2411-212: This combined action generates unique topological phases.
elements.sent-2411-213: The system we choose is the triple WSM, which has a $C_{6}$ rotational symmetry, having a non-linear dispersion.
elements.sent-2411-214: However, once the BCL light is switched on, the light modifies the rotational symmetry and in a combined effect of non-hermiticity and the BCL, we eventually land up in a double nodal ring and self-linked nodal line semimetals.
elements.sent-2411-215: This is the novelty of this work.
elements.sent-2411-216: The BCL light can be thought of as an important tool to switch between triple WSM to different nodal structures.
elements.sent-2411-217: We have also discussed the topological nature of the structures by analysing the Berry curvature.
elements.sent-2411-218: The important question in this regard is: can the combined action of the NH term and BCL light generate similar Fermi surfaces for charge 1 WSM? The answer is No. This is since the BCL significantly modifies the spatial inversion and rotational symmetry of the system.
elements.sent-2411-219: The multi-WSM, itself has a rotational symmetry of $C_{n ,$ this when modified by BCL put forward interesting scenario.. Acknowledgments: D.C.}

elements.figure-2411-012: \label{Fig6} \includegraphics[width=0.4\textwidth]{Fig6.pdf} \caption{${\cal E}_{\pm}$ vs $k_{x}$ and $k_{y}$ plots are presented.
